title: UNCCD Youth Caucus Call for Youth Consultants 2026 (Paid Opportunity for Young Researchers)
summary: The UNCCD Youth Caucus is inviting applications for youth consultants to support a global assessment on youth engagement in the implementation of the United Nations Convention to Combat Desertification (UNCCD). This is a paid, short-term consultancy opportunity running from March to June 2026. It is designed for young professionals with experience in research, data collection, and youth engagement who are interested in contributing to global environmental policy processes. About the Opportunity The UNCCD Youth Caucus aims to: Assess youth engagement in the implementation of the UNCCD Evaluate the effectiveness of the Youth Engagement Strategy Identify best practices and lessons learned Provide recommendations to strengthen meaningful youth participation Contribute to a report submitted to the Conference of the Parties (COP) under Decision 5/COP 16 Selected consultants will contribute to shaping evidence-based recommendations that inform global decision-making on youth participation in climate and environmental governance. Key Responsibilities Selected youth consultants will: Support the development and circulation of surveys Organise and facilitate consultations with young people Conduct interviews and focus group discussions Provide rapporteur support during meetings and consultations Assist with data analysis, synthesis, and visualization Contribute to drafting the final report for submission to COP Benefits Paid consultancy (details shared with shortlisted candidates) Experience working within a UN-related global process Opportunity to contribute to high-level policy and reporting Strengthening of research, facilitation, and analytical skills Engagement with an international network of youth leaders and experts Eligibility Criteria Applicants should have: Experience in data collection, analysis, and visualization Strong research and writing skills (with work samples) Knowledge or interest in youth engagement within UN processes (UNCCD experience is an advantage) A proactive mindset with the ability to contribute innovative ideas Ability to work collaboratively in a diverse, global team Age requirement: Applicants must be below 35 years old Timeline Consultancy period: March to June 2026 Application: Open now Why You Should Apply This opportunity provides a platform to: Influence global environmental and climate policy discussions Support evidence-based youth engagement strategies Build experience within international development and UN processes Contribute to advancing meaningful youth participation globally How to Apply Interested applicants should complete the official application form provided by the UNCCD Youth Caucus. Application link: https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSex6WbmJ_xgSO0Z_XK7gpchoYn2F0fJTFcOFL3qWzfqWi2M5Q/viewform Explore More Opportunities For more scholarships, internships, fellowships, and grants: https://opportunitiesforyouth.org The post UNCCD Youth Caucus Call for Youth Consultants 2026 (Paid Opportunity for Young Researchers) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Apply for the Fully Funded MOOD for Climate Action Program 2026 (Global Youth Opportunity)
summary: Meaningful Open Opportunities for Discovery (MOOD) for Climate Action is a transformative, fully funded program designed to empower young people to take meaningful action against climate change. This experiential learning journey equips participants with the knowledge, skills, and confidence needed to move from climate anxiety to climate action—while building impactful projects and joining a global network of changemakers. From Climate Anxiety to Climate Action Climate change is no longer a distant concern—it is a present and urgent global challenge. Yet, many educators and young people feel unprepared to address it effectively. Studies show that while most teachers recognize the importance of climate education, fewer than half feel equipped to teach it. At the same time, a significant majority of young people express fear about the future and concern over global inaction. The MOOD for Climate Action program responds to this gap by going beyond theory. It focuses on empowering learners not only to understand climate challenges but also to take meaningful, localized action while managing the emotional impact of the crisis. Rather than viewing climate change solely as a crisis, this program reframes it as an opportunity—for self-discovery, collaboration, innovation, and leadership. What the MOOD Journey Offers This program is a highly scalable and immersive learning experience designed to build agency and real-world impact. Participants will: Discover their personal purpose and how they can contribute to the world Gain a systemic understanding of climate challenges and solutions Take action on a local climate-related issue Build essential lifelong skills, including: Systems thinking Leadership and collaboration Social entrepreneurship Critical thinking Cross-cultural teamwork Emotional intelligence and regulation Project design and management Join a global community of like-minded young changemakers Enhance their CV with a project showcase and certification Each completed module earns a certificate issued by a United Nations University (UNU) Hub , adding strong international recognition to participants’ profiles. Who Can Apply? The MOOD for Climate Action program is open to: Young adults aged 15 to 35 years Individuals available for at least 5 hours per week Those passionate about climate action and personal development Applicants willing to actively participate in collaborative projects Individuals with a stable internet connection for virtual sessions Participants with proficiency in English for global collaboration Program Structure: 3 Certified Modules Participants can choose to complete one, two, or all three modules depending on their availability and goals. Each module includes certification upon completion. Key Highlights: Each module = 1 certification from a UNU Hub Flexible participation based on your schedule Top-performing projects receive incubation support from the INCO incubator to ensure long-term sustainability A Holistic Learning Experience The MOOD program is built around three core dimensions: 1. Thematic (Knowledge) Participants explore: Causes and impacts of climate change Climate justice and inequalities Sustainable solutions and carbon reduction strategies Adaptation approaches across different global regions Systems thinking and long-term climate foresight 2. Socio-Emotional (Self) This dimension focuses on personal development through: Self-awareness exercises Emotional regulation techniques Group collaboration and communication skills 3. Action (Impact) Participants gain hands-on experience by: Identifying local climate challenges Conducting fieldwork and interviews Designing and refining solutions Developing entrepreneurial and project implementation skills Pedagogical Infrastructure Learning Experience The program combines flexibility with high-quality support and interactive learning tools. Workload 12 hours of live workshops and mentoring per module Minimum 24 hours total commitment per module Flexible schedule compatible with studies, jobs, or internships Expert Support Participants learn from a diverse team of experts, including: Psychologists (for emotional and mental resilience) Social entrepreneurs (for project development) Climatologists (for scientific insights) Dedicated mentors for project-based modules Digital Learning Platform Interactive, AI-enhanced learning environment Real-time collaboration with global peers Continuous community engagement and support Certification Career Benefits Upon completing each module, participants receive a certification from a United Nations University Hub in France , enhancing their academic and professional profiles. Additionally, participants: Build a portfolio-ready climate project Gain international exposure Access opportunities for project incubation and scaling Why You Should Apply If you are passionate about climate action and want to move beyond awareness into real-world impact, this program provides the tools, network, and support you need. “In the MOOD for Climate Action” is more than a course—it’s a launchpad for the next generation of climate leaders. How to Apply Interested candidates can: Apply to the standard program Apply for a fully funded cohort Register Here, to begin their journey For more information, applicants are encouraged to visit the official program page or contact the organizers directly. Climate change demands urgent and collective action. Programs like MOOD for Climate Action are bridging the gap between knowledge and action by empowering young people with the skills, mindset, and opportunities needed to drive real change. If you are ready to become a climate changemaker, this could be your moment. Visit here for more details about the Learning institute. Discover more similar opportunities by clicking Here . title: Virginia Governor’s Fellows Program 2026: Paid Eight-Week Fellowship in the Office of the Governor for Future Public Service Leaders
summary: The Virginia Governor’s Fellows Program 2026 offers an exceptional opportunity for students passionate about public service, policy development, and government leadership to gain first-hand experience working within the Office of the Governor of Virginia . Designed to cultivate the next generation of public leaders, this prestigious program provides selected fellows with an immersive eight-week professional experience inside the Governor’s Administration . Participants contribute to real policy initiatives, support daily government operations, and collaborate with experienced policymakers while building valuable professional skills. The program provides an inside look at how state government functions and gives participants an opportunity to engage with the processes that shape policies impacting millions of citizens across the Commonwealth of Virginia. Participants also receive a stipend for their two-month service , making this fellowship both a professional development opportunity and a paid experience in public service. Overview of the Virginia Governor’s Fellows Program The Governor’s Fellows Program places participants directly within the Governor’s Office and related administrative units , where they assist with key initiatives that support governance and public administration. Fellows work closely with policy advisors, senior administrators, and other government professionals while contributing to projects that advance the priorities of the Commonwealth. This program provides fellows with: Hands-on experience in state government operations Exposure to public policy development and implementation Professional mentorship from government leaders Opportunities to collaborate with fellow participants on impactful policy initiatives The program is designed to equip participants with the knowledge, skills, and professional connections necessary for careers in public service, policy, law, governance, and civic leadership . Fellowship Structure and Learning Components The Virginia Governor’s Fellows Program combines practical work experience with structured professional development activities . Fellows not only contribute to daily operations but also complete several program components that deepen their understanding of government and policy. 1. Office Placement and Administrative Work Each fellow is placed within an office in the Governor’s Administration based on their academic background, interests, and professional goals. In their assigned offices, fellows may assist with: Policy research and analysis Administrative and operational support Legislative and policy briefings Public service initiatives Special projects relevant to the administration’s priorities This placement ensures that participants gain meaningful exposure to how state government functions on a day-to-day basis. 2. Professional Portfolio Development During the fellowship, participants develop a professional portfolio that highlights their work and growth throughout the program. The portfolio may include: Research outputs Policy analysis contributions Reports or briefings completed during the program Reflections on professional development and learning experiences This portfolio serves as a valuable professional asset that fellows can use in future career and academic opportunities. 3. Policy Project Another central component of the fellowship is the policy project . Fellows work in small teams to research and develop solutions for policy issues affecting the Commonwealth of Virginia. The policy project includes: Identifying a relevant policy challenge Conducting research and data analysis Developing policy recommendations Presenting findings to administration officials At the conclusion of the fellowship, fellows present their projects in a formal policy briefing , demonstrating their analytical skills and ability to develop actionable solutions. Key Benefits of the Program The Virginia Governor’s Fellows Program offers numerous benefits that support participants’ academic, professional, and leadership development. Professional Experience in Government Participants gain valuable insights into the inner workings of state government , policy development, and administrative operations. Paid Fellowship Experience Each fellow receives a stipend for their two-month service to the Commonwealth of Virginia. Policy Development Experience Through the policy project and office assignments, fellows develop practical skills in: Policy analysis Government research Strategic problem solving Public sector communication Networking and Mentorship Fellows have opportunities to connect with: Senior government officials Policy advisors Public service leaders Fellow students interested in governance and public administration These connections can help launch careers in government, policy, law, and public leadership . Who Should Apply The Governor’s Fellows Program welcomes applicants who demonstrate strong commitment to public service and leadership. Eligible applicants include: Rising college seniors Graduating seniors Graduate students Applicants must also meet one of the following conditions: Be enrolled at a Virginia college or university , or Be a Virginia resident studying at an out-of-state institution Applicants should demonstrate: Academic excellence Leadership potential Civic engagement and community service Participation in extracurricular activities and initiatives The program seeks candidates who are motivated, thoughtful, and eager to contribute to the work of the Commonwealth. Program Timeline Applicants should carefully review the timeline for the 2026 fellowship cycle. Application Deadline April 10, 2026 All application materials must be submitted by this date. Late or incomplete submissions will not be considered. Interview Phase April 21, 2026 Selected applicants will participate in virtual interviews with program officials. Final Decision Notifications April 30, 2026 All applicants will receive notification regarding their application status. Fellowship Program Dates June 8 – July 31, 2026 The fellowship is conducted full-time and in person during this period. Participants must be available for the entire duration of the program. Application Requirements Applicants must submit a complete application package through the official online portal. Required application materials include: Completed online application form Résumé outlining academic and professional experience Official or unofficial academic transcript Two letters of recommendation from academic or professional references Essay explaining commitment to public service and goals for participation Selection of up to three areas of interest for placement consideration These materials allow the selection committee to evaluate candidates based on their qualifications, leadership potential, and commitment to public service. Selection Process Fellows are selected through a competitive merit-based review process . The program follows equal opportunity principles and does not discriminate based on: Race Sex Color National origin Religion Sexual orientation Gender identity Age Political affiliation Disability Veteran status Applicants are evaluated based on academic performance, leadership potential, civic engagement, and commitment to public service. Career Impact of the Governor’s Fellows Program Many past fellows credit the program as a launchpad for successful careers in government and policy leadership . Participants gain: Professional experience in high-level policymaking environments Mentorship from government leaders Exposure to real public policy challenges Networking opportunities that support long-term career development Several alumni have gone on to serve in influential roles within state government, federal agencies, and policy organizations . How to Apply Interested applicants can learn more and submit their application through the Official Governor of Virginia website. Applicants are encouraged to prepare their materials early and ensure all required documents are submitted before the deadline. Click HERE for more information about the Virginia Governor’s Fellows Program 2026. Discover more global Fellowship opportunities on OFY HERE. The post Virginia Governor’s Fellows Program 2026: Paid Eight-Week Fellowship in the Office of the Governor for Future Public Service Leaders appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Apply for the Key Habitats Program 2026 — Funding & Support for Conservation and Community Initiatives in the Caribbean
summary: Conservationists, community leaders, NGOs, and environmental project teams in the Caribbean now have a valuable opportunity to apply for the Key Habitats Program 2026 , a funding initiative designed to support projects that conserve critical ecosystems and promote sustainable, community‑driven solutions. Led by the Dutch Caribbean Nature Alliance (DCNA) , the program provides financial support, technical guidance, and collaborative networks to help protect and restore biodiversity, strengthen local conservation capacity, and integrate community needs with environmental stewardship. ( dcnanature.org ) The Key Habitats Program targets initiatives that address priority conservation challenges, empowering local stakeholders to lead environmental action while contributing to long‑term ecosystem resilience across the Dutch Caribbean. About DCNA and the Key Habitats Program The Dutch Caribbean Nature Alliance is a regional partnership of nature conservation organizations across the Dutch Caribbean islands, including Aruba, Bonaire, Curaçao, Saba, Sint Eustatius, and Sint Maarten. DCNA works to protect biodiversity, strengthen institutional capacity, and ensure sustainable use of natural resources. The Key Habitats Program serves as a strategic funding mechanism that facilitates impactful, community‑linked conservation projects. The initiative prioritizes efforts that are guided by scientific evidence and develop lasting environmental and social benefits. By providing targeted funding and tailored support, the program helps emerging and established conservation efforts realize measurable outcomes. Why This Opportunity Matters The Caribbean region is home to some of the world’s most unique and vulnerable ecosystems, including coral reefs, mangroves, and tropical forests. These habitats are crucial not only for biodiversity but also for the livelihoods, food security, and cultural heritage of communities. However, pressures such as climate change, habitat degradation, invasive species, and unsustainable use of resources threaten these ecosystems and the services they provide. The Key Habitats Program supports locally led efforts that address these challenges by strengthening institutional capacity, elevating community voices in conservation, and providing tools and resources for impactful action. For conservation practitioners, community leaders, and environmental advocates, this program offers a platform to implement meaningful change that resonates across ecological and social systems. What the Program Offers The Key Habitats Program provides a combination of financial resources, technical support, and collaborative engagement designed to elevate conservation projects from concept to action. Financial Support Selected applicants receive project funding that can be used to implement conservation activities, build capacity, or support community‑driven solutions. Funding helps cover essential project costs such as: Fieldwork and data collection Community education or outreach materials Equipment and supplies Training and facilitation activities This financial support enables organizations and initiatives to execute conservation plans that might otherwise be limited by resource constraints. Technical and Organizational Support In addition to funding, the program offers technical guidance from conservation specialists and professionals. This may include support with: Project planning and design Monitoring and evaluation Data analysis and reporting Communications and stakeholder engagement By connecting teams with technical expertise, DCNA ensures that projects are both scientifically sound and culturally relevant to local communities. Networking and Collaboration The Key Habitats Program encourages collaboration across the region. Participants gain access to: Regional conservation networks Peer learning opportunities Knowledge exchange with practitioners and experts Support from partner organizations in the Dutch Caribbean These connections help strengthen long‑term capacity and foster partnerships that extend beyond the duration of individual projects. Who Should Apply The Key Habitats Program is open to a range of applicants working on conservation and sustainability initiatives within the Dutch Caribbean region. Eligible applicants include: Nonprofit organizations engaged in conservation or community development Community groups or cooperatives with environmental projects Research teams working on habitat protection, restoration, or biodiversity studies Youth‑led or community‑led initiatives with a focus on sustainability Projects may vary in scope, but successful applications typically demonstrate a clear conservation objective, measurable outcomes, and strong community engagement. Areas of Focus and Project Priorities The Key Habitats Program prioritizes projects that contribute to the conservation of critical ecosystems and support local community resilience. Priority areas include: Habitat protection and restoration Biodiversity monitoring and research Community engagement and environmental education Sustainable livelihood integration with conservation goals Projects that link environmental outcomes to community benefits — such as sustainable fishing practices, ecotourism, or food security solutions — are particularly encouraged. By emphasizing both ecological and social dimensions, the program seeks to create holistic approaches that benefit both nature and people. Application Process and Timeline Interested applicants should review the Key Habitats Program guidelines and submit their proposals through the official DCNA application process. Applications typically require: A clear project description Objectives and measurable outcomes Budget and resource plan Letters of support or partner endorsements While DCNA publishes specific timelines and deadlines for each funding round, applicants are encouraged to prepare materials well in advance to ensure completeness and competitive quality. Application Deadline: 13 April 2026 Applicants should consult the official DCNA Key Habitats Program page for more submission details . Explore More Opportunities for Youth Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities. title: Applications Open for Claude Community Ambassadors Program 2026
summary: Deadline: Rolling Applications Anthropic has opened applications for the Claude Community Ambassadors Program , a global initiative designed to support individuals who want to build and lead local communities around Claude. The program invites community builders, technical users, and AI enthusiasts to host events, share knowledge, and contribute to shaping the future of AI tools. The initiative focuses on empowering individuals to create vibrant local ecosystems where builders, developers, and curious learners can come together to explore and experiment with Claude. By supporting grassroots community efforts, Anthropic aims to expand access to AI knowledge while fostering collaboration across regions. About the Claude Community Ambassadors Program The Claude Community Ambassadors Program is built around the idea that strong communities drive innovation. Ambassadors are expected to organise meetups, workshops, and hackathons in their cities, creating spaces where people can learn, build, and exchange ideas. Participants are not required to be professional developers. While applicants should have meaningful experience using Claude tools such as Claude Code or Claude Cowork, the program places greater emphasis on community involvement and the ability to bring people together. This makes the opportunity accessible to a wide range of individuals, including educators, organisers, and tech enthusiasts. What Ambassadors Receive Selected ambassadors gain access to a range of resources to support their community-building efforts. These include: Funding support for local events such as meetups and hackathons Ready-to-use content and promotional assistance Swag and materials to enhance community engagement Monthly API credits to run demos and prototype ideas In addition, ambassadors receive early access to pre-release features and can participate in Builders Council sessions, where they share feedback directly with Anthropic’s product teams. Global and Flexible Participation The program is open to applicants from anywhere in the world, reflecting Anthropic’s focus on geographic diversity. There is no requirement to be based in a specific location, and multiple ambassadors can operate within the same city. This flexibility allows communities to grow organically across regions and ensures that participation is not limited by geography. Roles and Responsibilities Ambassadors are expected to take an active role in growing their local communities. This includes hosting events, running live demonstrations, and encouraging collaboration among participants. They are also responsible for sharing insights from their communities with Anthropic, helping shape future product development. Participants will join a private Slack network where they can connect with other ambassadors globally, exchange ideas, and collaborate on initiatives. Compensation and Benefits While the program does not provide direct financial compensation, it offers substantial support in other forms. Ambassadors receive event sponsorship, API credits, networking opportunities, promotional support, and recognition within the global AI community. These benefits are designed to help participants build impactful communities and expand their professional networks. Application Process Applications are open on a rolling basis, allowing interested individuals to apply at any time. Selected candidates will go through an onboarding process that includes signing an ambassador agreement, joining the private Slack workspace, and planning their first event. There is no cost to join the program, and all resources are provided by Anthropic. Why This Matters As AI continues to shape industries and societies, community-led initiatives play an important role in ensuring that knowledge and access are widely distributed. The Claude Community Ambassadors Program supports individuals who want to take the lead in making AI tools more accessible, collaborative, and locally relevant. For those passionate about community building and emerging technologies, the program offers a unique opportunity to contribute to a global network while creating impact at the local level. Deadline: Rolling Applications Application can be accessed from here . To know more about such opportunities, click here . The post Applications Open for Claude Community Ambassadors Program 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Apply for STIAS Fellowships 2027 — Fully Funded Research Fellowships in South Africa
summary: Are you a research scholar, scientist, or artist looking for dedicated time and resources to advance your work? The Stellenbosch Institute for Advanced Study (STIAS) is now accepting applications for it s 2027 Fellowship Programme. This fully funded fellowship provides scholars and artists with the financial support, interdisciplinary community, and protected time needed to make meaningful progress on ambitious research or creative projects. Located in Stellenbosch, South Africa, STIAS brings together international and local fellows to foster collaboration, innovation, and intellectual exchange across a wide range of disciplines. Whether your work is in the sciences, humanities, arts, or social sciences, STIAS provides the environment to deepen your research, produce new work, and connect with peers and mentors who can help amplify your impact. What the STIAS Fellowship Offers Full Financial Support Selected fellows receive comprehensive funding, including: A fully equipped offic e Weekday lunch living stipend Accommodation during the fellowship period Travel to and from Stellenbosch, South Africa This funding ensures fellows can dedicate themselves fully to their work without balancing additional employment or academic duties. Protected Research or Creative Time One of the greatest benefits of a STIAS fellowship is uninterrupted focus . Fellows are relieved of teaching, administrative, or other professional obligations, allowing them to: Conduct in-depth research or experiments Write academic publications, books, or reports Develop creative or artistic projects Experiment with interdisciplinary collaborations This protected time enables scholars and artists to tackle ambitious projects that may be difficult to achieve in a typical academic or professional setting. Interdisciplinary Engagement and Networking STIAS emphasizes cross-disciplinary collaboration . Fellows interact with a diverse community of scholars, scientists, and artists, exchanging ideas and methodologies. This fosters innovation and can open doors to new perspectives and opportunities. Attend seminars and workshops led by leading experts Engage in informal peer discussions and idea exchanges Build long-term professional networks locally, regionally, and internationally Career and Academic Development Beyond research and creative outputs, STIAS fellowships offer invaluable professional development: Opportunities to collaborate on international research projects Exposure to cutting-edge ideas across disciplines Mentorship from established scholars and practitioners Enhanced CV and professional profile for future grants, positions, or exhibitions Why This Opportunity Matters STIAS fellowships offer a rare chance to dedicate uninterrupted time to high-impact research or creative practice. For scholars, this can lead to publications, grant proposals, or collaborative projects that advance knowledge in their field. For artists, it can provide the time, resources, and community support to complete ambitious creative projects or develop new artistic directions. The programme also contributes to South Africa’s academic and cultural landscape by bringing global expertise to Stellenbosch, fostering collaborations with local institutions, and enhancing cross-disciplinary dialogue. Who Should Apply The STIAS Fellowship Programme is open to both emerging and established scholars, scientists, and artists . Ideal applicants include: Researchers with a strong record of publications or projects Scientists conducting advanced studies in their field Practicing artists or creative professionals with significant work in progress Individuals who benefit from interdisciplinary engagement and mentorship Applicants from any discipline are encouraged to apply, as long as the project will benefit from dedicated time, financial support, and STIAS’s collaborative environment. Application Process and Deadline Applicants should submit: A detailed research or creative proposal CV or portfolio References or letters of support Applications are accepted until the deadline listed on the official STIAS website. Fellows are selected based on the quality and feasibility of their projects, the potential impact of their work, and the alignment with STIAS’s interdisciplinary mission . Application Deadline: 30 April 2026 Learn more and apply on the official STIAS website. Explore More Opportunities for Youth Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities. title: Applications Open for Rooted and Rising: Youth Leaders Grant 2026
summary: Deadline: April 17, 2026 The Conrad Prebys Foundation has launched the Rooted and Rising: Youth Leaders Grant initiative, aimed at strengthening civic participation by investing in youth leadership programs across San Diego County. The initiative recognises that a healthy democracy depends on active engagement—yet many young people are still positioned as passive recipients rather than active contributors to change. This funding opportunity seeks to shift that narrative by supporting programs that equip young people with the skills, confidence, and platforms needed to engage meaningfully in civic life. About the Youth Leaders Grant 2026 The Rooted and Rising initiative is designed to support programs that combine leadership development with real-world civic engagement. By focusing on middle and high school-aged youth, the program aims to nurture a generation capable of shaping more inclusive and equitable communities. The foundation highlights that young people already bring creativity, resilience, and a strong desire to contribute. However, access to structured civic engagement opportunities remains uneven. Through this initiative, the foundation aims to bridge that gap and position youth as active partners in community problem-solving. In recent years, there has been growing recognition that early exposure to civic processes—such as dialogue, community organising, and collaborative decision-making—can significantly shape long-term democratic participation. Programs that engage youth at this stage not only build individual confidence but also strengthen collective capacity within communities, ensuring that future leaders are better equipped to address complex social challenges. Key Focus Areas Funded programs must integrate skill-building with practical experience. This includes: Leadership, collaboration, and problem-solving training Experiential learning tied to real community challenges such as climate resilience, arts and culture, health, and democratic participation Opportunities for youth to engage in storytelling, content creation, and visible leadership roles Strong mentorship networks connecting youth with civic leaders and career pathways A central requirement is that all programs culminate in an action project , where participants actively contribute to solving real community issues. Funding Details Grant Amount: Up to $150,000 per award Total Funding Pool: $2.5 million Number of Grants: Approximately 15–20 Grant Duration: July 2026 to December 2027 (18 months) Funding Type: Programmatic Eligibility Criteria Applicants must meet the following requirements: Be a 501(c)(3) nonprofit, fiscally sponsored organisation, or Tribal entity Operate in and/or serve San Diego County Focus on middle and/or high school-aged youth Deliver programs centred on leadership development, civic engagement, and community problem-solving Be an existing program that is expanding or strengthening its impact Programs must also demonstrate ongoing engagement rather than one-time activities and show clear outcomes such as participant numbers, skill development, and types of civic engagement activities. Program Expectations Proposals should include measurable outcomes and clearly defined program goals. Applicants are encouraged to highlight: Youth participation and leadership growth Civic engagement activities such as volunteering, advisory councils, or peer-led initiatives Use of youth-generated content like videos or social media storytelling Inclusion of diverse perspectives and community-informed approaches Preference will be given to programs that involve youth as co-designers or decision-makers and demonstrate strong community impact through structured frameworks or learning models. Application Process and Timeline Application Opens: March 9, 2026 Office Hours: March 17 – April 16, 2026 Application Deadline: April 17, 2026 (5:00 PM) Notification of Results: Late May to early June 2026 Grant Period: July 2026 – December 2027 Applicants must register on the foundation’s grant portal before applying. Registration approval may take 2–3 days, so early preparation is recommended. Why This Matters By investing in youth leadership, the initiative aims to strengthen not just individual participants but the broader civic ecosystem of San Diego. The program envisions a future where young people see themselves as stakeholders in their communities—capable of driving meaningful, long-term change. Organisations working at the intersection of youth development and civic engagement are encouraged to apply and contribute to building a more participatory and resilient society. Deadline: April 17, 2026 Application can be accessed from here. To know more about such opportunities, click here . title: Apply for the HealthyDiets4Africa Agribusiness Accelerator Program
summary: Applications are now open for the HealthyDiets4Africa Agribusiness Accelerator Program (Cohort III), an initiative designed to support youth and women innovators working to strengthen food systems across Africa. The program focuses on early-stage agribusiness entrepreneurs who are developing solutions to improve food security, nutrition, and sustainability. By combining training, mentorship, and market access opportunities, the accelerator helps participants transform their ideas into scalable and investment-ready ventures. About the Program The HealthyDiets4Africa Agribusiness Accelerator is part of a broader effort to promote innovation in agriculture and food systems across the continent. It targets young innovators and women entrepreneurs who are building solutions that address challenges in food production, processing, distribution, and nutrition. The program provides a structured environment where participants can refine their ideas, strengthen their business models, and gain practical knowledge on how to grow and sustain their ventures. Through both virtual and in-person components, participants are exposed to real-world insights and industry expertise. Why This Opportunity Matters Across Africa, food systems continue to face significant challenges, including limited access to nutritious food, inefficiencies in value chains, and the impacts of climate change. At the same time, there is growing recognition of the role that young people and women can play in driving innovation in agriculture. The HealthyDiets4Africa Agribusiness Accelerator addresses this gap by providing targeted support to early-stage innovators. By equipping participants with the skills, networks, and visibility they need, the program helps turn promising ideas into practical solutions that can create lasting impact. What Participants Will Gain One of the key strengths of the program is its focus on practical support and capacity building. Participants will take part in structured training sessions covering essential areas such as business development, value chain analysis, and market positioning. These sessions are designed to help innovators better understand how to build viable and competitive agribusinesses. In addition to training, participants will receive mentorship from experienced professionals and industry experts. This guidance helps them refine their ideas, address challenges, and make informed decisions about scaling their ventures. The program also provides opportunities for market exposure. Selected participants may showcase their innovations at trade fairs, exhibitions, and networking events, allowing them to connect with potential partners, customers, and investors. Financial support is another important benefit. Top-performing participants will be eligible for awards from a total prize pool of up to $5,000. While modest, this funding can help early-stage innovators test or expand their solutions. More importantly, the program also helps participants position themselves to access additional funding and investment opportunities beyond the accelerator. Who Can Apply The accelerator is open to individuals and teams who meet the following criteria: Young people between the ages of 18 and 35 Women entrepreneurs Early-stage agribusiness innovators, typically within the first two years of operation Applicants should have a clear idea, prototype, or early-stage business that demonstrates potential for growth and impact. The program is particularly interested in solutions that can be scaled and contribute to improving food systems. Eligible Areas of Innovation The program welcomes a wide range of agribusiness and food system innovations. These may include: Sustainable agriculture and crop production Urban and peri-urban farming solutions Food processing and value addition Post-harvest handling and storage innovations Distribution systems and market access solutions Projects may focus on crops, livestock, or nutrition-related innovations, as long as they contribute to healthier and more sustainable food systems. Application Process Applicants are required to complete an online application form and submit a short pitch, which may be in video or audio format. The pitch should clearly explain the problem being addressed, the proposed solution, and its potential impact. Interested applicants are encouraged to apply as early as possible, as the selection process is competitive. You can learn more and apply on the HealthyDiets4Africa Project website. Application deadline: 22 March 2026 Explore More Opportunities for Youth Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities. The post Apply for the HealthyDiets4Africa Agribusiness Accelerator Program appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Apply for the Google.org AI for Government Innovation Challenge 2026 (Up to $3 Million + Global Accelerator Support)
summary: Organizations worldwide can now apply for the Google.org Impact Challenge: AI for Government Innovation 2026 , a major global funding opportunity supporting projects that use artificial intelligence to improve public services and address real societal challenges. With a total funding pool of $30 million , this open call invites nonprofits, social enterprises, and academic institutions to develop scalable, AI-powered solutions in partnership with governments . Selected organizations can receive between $1 million and $3 million or more , along with access to the Google.org Accelerator , technical mentorship, and cloud resources. About the Google.org AI for Government Innovation Challenge The Google.org Impact Challenge is designed to support projects that use cutting-edge AI technologies , including generative and agentic AI, to solve complex public service challenges. The initiative focuses on solutions that deliver tangible, measurable improvements in people’s lives , rather than projects limited to internal government operations. Organizations must work closely with government partners to ensure their solutions can be implemented in real-world systems and used by public institutions over the long term. Why This Opportunity Matters Governments around the world face increasing pressure to deliver efficient, accessible, and inclusive services. At the same time, AI technologies are evolving rapidly, offering new ways to solve complex public challenges. However, many organizations lack the funding, technical capacity, or partnerships needed to bring these solutions to life. The Google.org Impact Challenge addresses this gap by combining large-scale funding, technical expertise, and global collaboration . For organizations working at the intersection of technology, public policy, and social impact , this is a rare opportunity to build solutions that can improve lives at scale. What Selected Organizations Receive This opportunity goes beyond funding by offering a full ecosystem of support to help organizations build, test, and scale impactful solutions. Significant Grant Funding Selected organizations may receive: $1 million to $3 million or more per project Funding that can be used over up to 36 months Support for developing, testing, and scaling AI solutions This level of funding allows organizations to move from idea or prototype stage to real-world implementation. Access to the Google.org Accelerator All selected participants will join the Google.org Accelerator , a multi-month global program designed to advance AI-driven projects. The accelerator includes: A dedicated Project Success Manager A technical AI Coach from Google Structured training on the AI product lifecycle One-on-one mentorship from Google experts Peer learning with other global organizations The program begins in Fall 2026 and includes both virtual sessions and in-person events, including a final Demo Day in December 2026 . Google Cloud Credits and AI Tools Participants may also receive: Up to $200,000 in Google Cloud credits (based on eligibility) Access to advanced tools such as Vertex AI and Gemini Technical infrastructure to build and deploy AI solutions These resources help organizations accelerate development and scale their solutions effectively. Focus Areas for Projects The challenge prioritizes projects that apply AI to improve public services in key areas: Health Projects that improve access to healthcare services, strengthen frontline systems, or support preventive care at scale. Resilience Solutions that help communities prepare for, respond to, and recover from crises using real-time data and predictive tools. Economy Initiatives that improve public infrastructure, expand access to services, and create pathways for economic opportunity. While these are priority areas, the program also accepts innovative projects outside these categories , as long as they align with the overall goal of improving public services. Who Can Apply The challenge is open globally to organizations that have a clear social impact focus. Eligible applicants include: Nonprofit organizations Social enterprises (including for-profit companies with a strong social impact mission) Academic and research institutions Applicants must: Apply as an organization (individuals are not eligible) Have (or be able to secure) a partnership with a government entity Submit applications in English Demonstrate a clear plan for implementation and impact Government partnership is a key requirement, as projects must be integrated into real public systems. What Makes a Strong Application According to the official criteria, successful proposals should demonstrate: A clear and urgent public service challenge A human-centered approach that considers real user needs Use of AI as a core component , not just an add-on A feasible implementation plan with measurable outcomes Strong government collaboration Potential to scale and replicate across regions Projects must also show how they will deliver real, measurable benefits to communities , not just internal system improvements. Application Deadline Application Deadline: April 3, 2026 (11:59 PM PT) Applicants are encouraged to start early, especially to secure or formalize government partnerships where needed. Learn more and apply on the official Google.org website. Explore More Opportunities for Youth Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities. The post Apply for the Google.org AI for Government Innovation Challenge 2026 (Up to $3 Million + Global Accelerator Support) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Wellcome Research Contract Opportunities 2026: Funding for Mental Health Research Collaboration and Research Culture Evaluation
summary: Wellcome, one of the world’s leading philanthropic foundations dedicated to improving global health through research and innovation, has opened two major contract opportunities aimed at strengthening research ecosystems and advancing knowledge in critical areas of health science. Through these opportunities, Wellcome seeks external organisations, research institutions, and experts capable of delivering high-quality analytical and evaluation work that will inform future strategies in mental health research collaboration and research culture transformation. The two open contract opportunities focus on: Improving infrastructure for lived experience collaboration in mental health research Independent evaluation of research culture change initiatives funded by Wellcome Both opportunities are part of Wellcome’s broader mission to fund discovery research and generate solutions to major global health challenges including mental health, infectious disease, and climate-related health risks. These contract opportunities offer organisations the chance to collaborate with Wellcome while contributing valuable insights that can shape future research policies, funding models, and institutional practices. About Wellcome’s Global Research Mission Wellcome is internationally recognised for supporting scientific discovery and innovation that improves health outcomes worldwide. The organisation invests in research that addresses urgent global challenges while promoting equity, collaboration, and sustainability in research systems. Wellcome’s strategic priorities focus on three key areas: Mental Health Infectious Disease Climate and Health Beyond funding research itself, Wellcome also invests in initiatives that improve research culture, strengthen collaboration between institutions, and create environments where researchers can thrive. The two contract opportunities currently open reflect these priorities by addressing: Collaboration between researchers and individuals with lived experience in mental health Evaluation of institutional initiatives aimed at improving research culture Contract Opportunity 1 Improving Infrastructure for Lived Experience Collaboration in Mental Health Research Wellcome is seeking a qualified supplier to conduct a detailed analysis of how research institutions support collaboration with individuals who have lived experience of mental health challenges. The aim of this project is to understand how research institutions incorporate lived experience perspectives into mental health research processes, structures, and decision-making. This work will help Wellcome identify best practices and challenges in integrating lived experience into research environments. Purpose of the Project The selected organisation will be responsible for mapping and analysing institutional approaches that facilitate collaboration with people who have lived experience relevant to mental health research. The project will primarily focus on institutions in the United Kingdom, though some international examples may also be included. The analysis should examine: Institutional practices that support lived experience engagement The development of structures that enable collaboration How organisations achieved internal support and buy-in Lessons learned and challenges encountered Opportunities for improving lived experience collaboration Examples of Institutional Practices to be Studied The research should investigate a range of existing models and practices used by research institutions, including: Lived experience advisory panels that help develop funding applications Institutional support systems that help research projects integrate lived experience perspectives Mentorship and career support structures for researchers with lived experience Frameworks that enable meaningful participation of community members in research projects Expected Outcomes The selected supplier will generate insights that help Wellcome and research institutions better understand how to: Design inclusive mental health research environments Support researchers with lived experience Strengthen collaboration between academic researchers and communities Improve the relevance and impact of mental health research Download the Full RFP PDF Submission Deadline 17 April 2026 Contract Opportunity 2 Independent Evaluation of Research Culture Change Initiatives Wellcome is also seeking an independent organisation to conduct a comprehensive evaluation of initiatives funded through the Institutional Fund for Research Culture (IFRC). These initiatives aim to foster healthier, more inclusive, and collaborative research environments across UK institutions. Through this contract, Wellcome seeks to generate evidence-based insights that can guide the future development of equitable research ecosystems. Background of the Initiative Through the Institutional Fund for Research Culture, Wellcome supported UK institutions in experimenting with new strategies that promote: Cultural change in research environments Collaboration across disciplines Equity and inclusivity in research systems Improved wellbeing for researchers Alongside the funding initiative, Wellcome also established the Institutional Research Culture Community (IRCC), a collaborative network bringing together participating institutions. The IRCC was designed to enable institutions to: Share learning and experiences Support one another in implementing culture change initiatives Build cross-institutional collaborations Objectives of the Evaluation The selected supplier will conduct an independent evaluation of both the IFRC and IRCC initiatives. The main goal is to generate actionable insights that inform future strategies of Wellcome’s UK Equitable Research Ecosystems team. The evaluation should examine: The effectiveness of funded culture change initiatives The impact of the Institutional Research Culture Community Lessons learned from participating institutions Recommendations for future funding and policy development Importance of the Evaluation This evaluation is essential to understanding how research culture initiatives can: Improve working conditions for researchers Foster diversity and equity in academic environments Strengthen collaboration between institutions Enhance the long-term sustainability of research ecosystems The findings will play a critical role in shaping Wellcome’s future funding strategies and policy priorities. Download the Full RFP PDF Download the supplier questions and answers PDF Submission Deadline 7 April 2026 Why These Opportunities Matter These contract opportunities represent a significant chance for organisations to contribute to improving the global research ecosystem. By participating in these projects, suppliers can help advance: Inclusive mental health research practices Stronger research culture across institutions Better collaboration between researchers and communities Evidence-based policies that support equitable research environments Organisations selected for these contracts will work closely with one of the most influential research funding organisations in the world while generating insights that could shape the future of global research systems. Eligibility and Participation Both contract opportunities are open to organisations and suppliers with the relevant expertise and experience required to complete the projects described in the Requests for Proposals (RFPs). Applicants are expected to demonstrate strong capabilities in areas such as: Research evaluation Policy analysis Mental health research collaboration Institutional research culture studies Data analysis and impact assessment Full eligibility requirements and submission instructions are outlined in the official Request for Proposal documents available through Wellcome’s procurement system. How to Apply Interested organisations must review the full Request for Proposal documents and submit their applications through the Oracle portal. The RFP documents include detailed information on: Project scope Deliverables Eligibility criteria Submission requirements Evaluation criteria Applicants are encouraged to carefully review the documents before submitting their proposals. For more information and access to the RFP documents, visit Here. Click HERE to discover more grant opportunities on OFY. The post Wellcome Research Contract Opportunities 2026: Funding for Mental Health Research Collaboration and Research Culture Evaluation appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .
